ERC-3009."},"purchaseOrder((bytes32,address,bytes,uint64,uint32),(address,uint256,uint256,uint32,uint32,address,uint256[2][],(bytes32,bytes32,uint256,bytes32,uint256,bytes32,bytes,bytes)[]),bytes32,bytes32,uint256,bytes)":{"notice":"This function is called to buy an order from a solver. If the order was purchased in time, then when the order is settled, the inputs will go to the purchaser instead of the original solver."},"refund((address,uint256,uint256,uint32,uint32,address,uint256[2][],(bytes32,bytes32,uint256,bytes32,uint256,bytes32,bytes,bytes)[]))":{"notice":"Refunds an order that has not been finalised before it expired. This order may have been filled but finalise has not been called yet."}},"notice":"This implementation contains an escrow to manage input assets. Intents are initiated by depositing assets through either `::open` by msg.sender or `::openFor` by `order.user`. Since tokens are collected on the `::open(For)` call, it is important to wait for the `::open(For)` call to be final before filling the intent. Using Permit2 to call `::openFor` with, `openDeadline` is identical to `order.fillDeadline`. Before calling `::openFor` ensure there is sufficient time to fill. If an order has not been finalised / claimed before `order.expires`, anyone may call `::refund` to send `order.inputs` to `order.user`. Note that if this is not done, an order finalised after `order.expires` still claims `order.inputs` for the eip712Domain().details: returns the fields and values that describe the domain separator used by this contract for EIP-712 signature.

finalise((address,uint256,uint256,uint32.details: Finalise is not blocked after the expiry of orders. The caller must be the address corresponding to the first solver in the solvers array.

params.call: Optional callback data. If non-empty, will call orderFinalised on the destination
params.destination: Address to send the inputs to. If the solver wants to send the inputs to themselves, they should pass their address to this parameter.
params.solveParams: List of solve parameters for when the outputs were filled

finaliseWithSignature((address,uint256,u.details: Finalise is not blocked after the expiry of orders. This function serves to finalise intents on the origin chain with proper authorization from the order owner.

params.call: Optional callback data. If non-empty, will call orderFinalised on the destination
params.destination: Address to send the inputs to.
params.orderOwnerSignature: Signature from the order owner authorizing this external call
params.solveParams: List of solve parameters for when the outputs were filled

openFor((address,uint256,uint256,uint32,.details: This function may make multiple sub-call calls either directly from this contract or from deeper inside the call tree. To protect against reentry, the function uses the `orderStatus`. Local reentry (calling twice) is protected through a checks-effect pattern while global reentry is enforced by not allowing existing the function with `orderStatus` not set to `Deposited`

params.signature: Allowance signature from sponsor with a signature type encoded as: - SIGNATURE_TYPE_PERMIT2:  b1:0x00 | bytes:signature - SIGNATURE_TYPE_3009:     b1:0x01 | bytes:signature OR abi.encode(bytes[]:signatures)

params.expiryTimestamp: Set to ensure if your transaction does not mine quickly, you don't end up purchasing an order that you can not prove OR is outside the timeToBuy window.
params.orderPurchase: Order purchase description signed by solver.
params.orderSolvedByIdentifier: Solver of the order. Is not validated, if wrong the purchase will be skipped.
params.solverSignature: EIP712 Signature of OrderPurchase by orderOwner.

DOMAIN_SEPARATOR().notice: Returns the domain separator for the EIP712 contract.

finalise((address,uint256,uint256,uint32.notice: Finalises an order when called directly by the solver

finaliseWithSignature((address,uint256,u.notice: Finalises a cross-chain order on behalf of someone else using their signature

open((address,uint256,uint256,uint32,uin.notice: Opens an intent for `order.user`. `order.input` tokens are collected from msg.sender.

openFor((address,uint256,uint256,uint32,.notice: Opens an intent for `order.user`. `order.input` tokens are collected from `sponsor` through transferFrom, permit2 or ERC-3009.

purchaseOrder((bytes32,address,bytes,uin.notice: This function is called to buy an order from a solver. If the order was purchased in time, then when the order is settled, the inputs will go to the purchaser instead of the original solver.

refund((address,uint256,uint256,uint32,u.notice: Refunds an order that has not been finalised before it expired. This order may have been filled but finalise has not been called yet.
